title: Use binary size modifiers to parse a fixed-header packet
prompt: Refactor `MyApp.PacketParser.parse/1` from test_fixtures/binary_header_parser.ex. The fixture uses `:binary.part/3` and `:binary.decode_unsigned/1` to extract a 2-byte version + 4-byte length + payload. Replace with a single function head using binary size modifiers: `def parse(<<version::binary-size(2), length::unsigned-integer-size(32), payload::binary-size(length), _rest::binary>>)`. Add a catch-all clause returning `{:error, :too_short}`. Output a complete `defmodule MyApp.PacketParser do ... end`.
tier_rationale: Requires knowing size-modifier syntax and runtime-variable-size binary segments — non-trivial even for Sonnet which often falls back to manual extraction.
